title: Quiz: Parameter Shift Rule
description: Test your understanding of parameter-shift gradients

questions.question: What is the parameter-shift rule formula for Pauli rotation gates?
questions.explanation: The parameter-shift rule for Pauli rotations is ∂f/∂θ = (1/2)[f(θ+π/2) - f(θ-π/2)]. The shift of π/2 is specific to the eigenvalue spectrum of Pauli operators.

questions.question: How many circuit evaluations are needed to compute the gradient of n parameters?
questions.explanation: For n parameters, the parameter-shift rule requires 2 circuit evaluations per parameter (θ+π/2 and θ-π/2), giving 2n total evaluations for the full gradient.

questions.question: Why is the shift value π/2 used for Pauli rotation gates?

options.text: It's arbitrary and any value works
It matches the eigenvalue gap of Pauli generators

questions.explanation: The shift value π/2 is derived from the eigenvalue spectrum of Pauli operators (±1/2). This specific shift transforms the sinusoidal dependence into its derivative.

questions.question: The parameter-shift rule gives exact gradients, not approximations.
questions.explanation: True. Unlike finite differences, the parameter-shift rule exploits the mathematical structure of quantum gates to give analytically exact gradients. Errors only come from shot noise.

questions.question: Which gates require a modified (four-term) parameter-shift rule?
questions.explanation: Controlled rotation gates (CRX, CRY, CRZ) have a different eigenvalue spectrum than single-qubit Pauli rotations, requiring a four-term parameter-shift rule.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of parameter-shift over finite differences?

options.text: No numerical error from step size choice

questions.explanation: Parameter-shift gives exact gradients with no step-size related numerical error. Finite differences require choosing ε, which can cause truncation or numerical instability.

questions.question: For which type of operation is the parameter-shift rule NOT applicable?

options.text: Arbitrary unitary with no clear generator structure

questions.explanation: The parameter-shift rule requires gates with specific generator structure (like Pauli operators). Arbitrary unitaries without this structure may not have a simple shift rule.

questions.question: Which statements about parameter-shift are true? (Select all)

options.text: Is based on the structure of Pauli rotation gates
Can compute Hessians with additional shifts

questions.explanation: Parameter-shift works on hardware (a), exploits Pauli gate structure (c), and can compute Hessians (d). It does NOT require wavefunction access - only measurement outcomes are needed.
